results.title: Ask questions with Gemini
results.summary: The webpage provides guidance on using the Gemini feature in Threat Intelligence for conducting natural language searches related to cybersecurity topics. Users can ask questions like "What is APT44?" to receive AI-generated summaries based on Google Threat Intelligence reports, focusing on threat actors, malware, and campaigns. The page emphasizes the importance of phrasing queries clearly and concisely for optimal results, suggesting specific questions for deeper insights. For example, queries like "What is the log4j vulnerability?" or "How do I detect alphv?" are recommended for effective information retrieval.
highlights:
# Ask questions with Gemini To simplify search, Gemini in Threat Intelligence redefines search with natural language. With this feature, you can ask a natural language question and quickly get AI-powered overviews of a topic. When you perform a single term search, such as, "what is APT44", you get the Gemini search summary. > Note: the Gemini summary is only displayed when there is information from the source data to answer the search query. > > Gemini in Threat Intelligence search currently only generates search summaries based on the Google Threat Intelligence reports, threat actor, malware, and campaign data. > 
> ⚠️ ## Suggested search queries

Use case

Suggested queries

Get an overview of threat actors "What is lockbit?"
"What is Scattered Spider?"

Deep dive into specific questions "What is the log4j vulnerability aka Log4Shell?"
"What are the indicators of killnet?"
"How do I detect alphv?" "What industries does APT29 target?" > - Keep questions concise and to the point. Avoid overly complex sentences or multiple questions in a single query. > - Asking specific questions such as "how does APT29 move laterally?" typically generates better responses. > - Phrase queries in a question format.
> 
> Tips on how to phrase your queries to get the most useful results:
> 
> 📘

results.title: Agentic Platform - Google Threat Intelligence - VirusTotal
results.summary:
The Agentic Platform from Google Threat Intelligence, available for Enterprise customers, provides a conversational interface powered by AI to enhance cybersecurity investigations. Users can interact with AI agents to ask questions, generate reports, retrieve details on indicators of compromise (IoCs), and conduct private scans for in-depth analysis. The platform supports multiple languages and allows for follow-up questions and conversation management. However, users are cautioned against submitting personal information and should verify critical information due to the potential for inaccuracies in AI responses. For more details, visit the [Agentic Platform documentation](https://gtidocs.virustotal.com/docs/agentic-platfo). 

If you are looking for information specifically about Google Gemini, please clarify your query, as the Agentic Platform is focused on threat intelligence and cybersecurity.

results.title: Alerts - Google Threat Intelligence - VirusTotal
results.summary:
The webpage provides a comprehensive guide on managing Alerts within Google Threat Intelligence's Digital Threat Monitoring (DTM) system. It explains how DTM continuously monitors the deep and dark web for mentions of specified topics or entities, generating Alerts when relevant content is detected. Users can view and manage these Alerts through the DTM interface, which includes features for filtering, triaging, and enriching Alerts to streamline response efforts.

Key functionalities include:
- **Viewing Alerts**: Users can access the Alert List by signing into the Google Threat Intelligence platform and navigating to the Digital Threat Monitoring tab.
- **Alert Details**: Each Alert includes an AI-generated summary, source information, and details about the matched content.
- **Email Notifications**: Users can configure email alerts for new or modified Alerts.

For more specific inquiries related to the Gemini platform, users may need to explore additional resources or support options provided by Google.

###### Start typing to search… # Alerts Digital Threat Monitoring (DTM) continuously ingests documents from the deep and dark web to search for mentions of topics or entities that you designate in Monitors. When content in a collected document matches the conditions that you define in one of your Monitors, an Alert is created. The new Alert is displayed in the DTM**Alerts**tab. DTM provides numerous capabilities to filter and triage these Alerts to help you focus on the ones that matter most. ... > 📘> Email notifications can be sent when Alerts are created or modified. results.title: Reports & Analysis - Google Threat Intelligence - VirusTotal
results.summary:
The "Reports & Analysis" module from Google Threat Intelligence on VirusTotal offers continuously updated, detailed reports on various cybersecurity topics. These reports are crucial for organizations looking to enhance their threat detection and incident response capabilities. They are generated in two ways: through community contributions and by Google Threat Intelligence analysts. 

Users can access a list view of reports, which can be sorted and filtered by attributes such as targeted industries and regions, allowing for tailored searches based on specific needs. The platform also supports advanced searches, TTP analysis, and the ability to follow reports for notifications. Additionally, users can request new OSINT reports and integrate report IoCs into their IoC streams.

For more information, visit the [Reports & Analysis module](https://gtidocs.virustotal.com/docs/reports-and-an).

This unique approach provides our users with the best of both worlds: highly specialized, expert-vetted analysis from Google's top threat experts, combined with the diverse, crowdsourced real-world insights and on-the-ground intelligence that only a broad and active community can provide. To access the Reports & Analysis module, simply click on the Reports & Analysis option in the platform's left-hand menu. results.title: Use cases and other resources - Google Threat Intelligence
results.summary:
The webpage on Google Threat Intelligence (Google TI) provides an overview of its use cases and resources aimed at enhancing security operations. It highlights how Google TI integrates data from Google’s infrastructure, Mandiant, and VirusTotal to offer insights into the global threat landscape. Key use cases include phishing and brand monitoring, incident response, vulnerability management, and advanced hunting, each supported by examples and video demonstrations. Additional resources include advanced search cheatsheets, a Livehunt starter pack, community engagement opportunities, and on-demand webinars. This content is designed to help security teams effectively utilize Google TI for threat detection and response. 

For specific information about Gemini, please clarify your query, as the provided page does not directly address it.
highlights:
Cases] Google Threat Intelligence 
**Documentation
Use cases and other resources
All
Pages ###### Start typing to search…
# Use cases and other resources Google Threat Intelligence (Google TI) equips security teams with a uniquely comprehensive understanding of the global threat landscape. By combining intelligence from Google’s infrastructure, Mandiant’s frontline expertise, and VirusTotal’s collaborative data, Google TI delivers the depth, breadth, and context needed to make faster, more informed security decisions. This section is designed to help you get started with the platform. Through real-world examples and short video demonstrations, you’ll learn how to apply Google TI to common use cases across threat detection, investigation, and response. Whether you're enriching alerts, analyzing phishing activity, or prioritizing vulnerabilities, these examples are here to support your work and help your team unlock more value from the platform. We’ll continue to expand this library ... ## Core Use Cases
 These use cases illustrate how Google TI supports practical, high-impact security operations. Each section includes ... * Phishing & Brand Monitoring Identify phishing campaigns, domain abuse, and impersonation targeting your organization or users. * Incident Response Accelerate investigations with enriched indicators, threat actor context, and infrastructure mapping. * Vulnerability Management Assess and prioritize vulnerabilities using real-world exploitation data and threat intelligence signals. * Advanced Hunting Search for suspicious activity using entity pivoting, pattern matching, and investigative tools.
## Other Resources 
* Advanced searches Cheatsheet A quick reference for advanced search operators, helping you pivot through VirusTotal's dataset to uncover connections between files, URLs, IPs, and domains, and to perform detailed threat hunting. * Livehunt Starter Pack 
A curated collection of YARA rules to help you get started with Livehunt. * Google Cloud Security Community Connect with other security professionals, ask questions, share best practices, and get product updates directly from the Google Threat Intelligence team. * Google TI Hunting and Use case Webinars Access on-demand webinars that demonstrate practical threat hunting techniques and real-world use cases. **Updated5 months ago

results.title: Malware & Tools - Google Threat Intelligence - VirusTotal
results.summary:
The webpage on "Malware & Tools" from Google Threat Intelligence provides a comprehensive overview of malware families and the tools associated with them. Users can filter and explore detailed information about various malware and tools based on criteria such as last modification date, object types, origin, targeted regions, and industries affected. 

Key features include the ability to follow specific malware or tools to monitor changes over time, view detailed profiles that include summaries, associations, and indicators of compromise (IOCs), and access news and analysis related to each malware. The platform is designed for users seeking in-depth intelligence on malware threats and their operational contexts.

For users interested in the Gemini platform, this resource may not directly relate, but it offers valuable insights into malware intelligence that could be relevant for security assessments or threat analysis in various applications, including those integrated with Gemini.

link> Google Threat Intelligence

# Malware & Tools Google Threat Intelligence lets you explore highly contextualized details about Malware families and the Tools used to leverage them. Malware and tools list. The Malware & Tools List will show items sorted by Relevance, you can filter them utilizing the Filters pane on the left column. ## Filter Malware & Tools
 In the**Filters**pane, select the desired filters based on the following options to view only the Malware or Tools you ... * **Last Modification date**: Date when the malware/toolkit was last updated with new associations, TTPs, IoC submissions, etc. * **Object Types**: Choose between listing Malware or Software Toolkits. * **Origin**: Select between items that were investigated and curated by Google Threat Intelligence analysts or by our partners. * **Source Regions**: Known/Suspected country or region of origin. * **Targeted Regions**: Known/Suspected country or region that was attacked with this malware/tool. ... In the**Malware & Tools list**, select one or multiple cards and click**Follow**button, for any Malware or Tool to monitor changes to selected entities over time, including new variations, associations, or reporting. Malware Follow button highlighted with red boxes To add a Malware or Tool to a Threat Profile, open the card details and use the Follow button inside, it will give you the option to choose which profile to add it to. Malware Follow and Add to Threat profile 
## View Malware Details
 Click on the name of any Malware or Tool card for a view of the Malware profile and its details, navigate its tabs for ... * **Details**: This tab displays the same comprehensive summary of the Malware or Tool profile as seen in the quick view. It also includes an Overview block with information such as Role, Operating systems affected, Aliases, lists the industries and regions known to be targeted, capabilities,detection names, dates on malware's activity and relevant tags. A list of news analysis reports related to the Malware is also displayed. The following blocks have summarized information on Associations, Reporting, News & Analysis, and Telemetry. Malware Follow and Add to Threat profile * **Associations**: This tab provides a list of the various associations with this Malware family. It includes other associated Malware, Toolkits, Threat Actors and Vulnerabilities. Various filter options let you customize your view. Associations tab * **IOCs**: This tab includes a table with all known Indicators attributed to this Malware family, such as specific IP ... IOCs tab * **Rules**: This tab shows detection rules for this Malware or Toolkits IoCs, there are Yara, Sigma and IDS rules, and they can be crowdsourced or curated. These rules can be downloaded for use in threat hunt efforts or other workflows ... Rules tab * **TTPs**: This tab shows the Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures (TTPs) observed to be associated with delivering, deploying, or executing the selected Malware. All TTPs associated with the Malware can be downloaded by clicking Download TTPs from the Actions drop-down. TTPs tab. 
Actions available for TTPs: * Hovering over the ID of a Technique or Tactic provides a brief description of it and a link to open details in MITRE ... * Clicking on the name of a Technique or Tactic will redirect you to a Intelligene search filtering for that specific Technique (example: attack\_technique:T1129) * Clicking on the matches tag on the bottom right corner of a Technique will redirect you to the list of the current Malware's IOCs that match this Technique * **Reporting**: This tab displays the latest reports generated by Google Threat Intelligence and crowdsourced articles talking about the Malware or Toolkit you are looking at. The items can be filtered by Modification date, Origin, Report Type, Source region, Targeted Region and Targeted Industry utilizing the Filters pane. results.title: Campaigns - Google Threat Intelligence - VirusTotal
results.summary:
The "Campaigns" feature in Google Threat Intelligence (VirusTotal) provides security teams with insights into active threat campaigns relevant to their industries and regions. This tool helps prioritize response actions by offering a campaign-centric view of emerging threats, updated daily with new data from analysts. 

Key functionalities include:

- **Campaign Creation**: Campaigns are developed based on threat actor analysis, motivations, and the impact on targeted organizations. The process ensures sensitive data is protected while providing actionable intelligence.
  
- **Campaign List**: Users can view all active campaigns, filter them by various criteria (e.g., creation date, targeted region, associated malware), and access detailed information about each campaign.

- **Campaign Card**: Each campaign is represented as a card containing essential details such as the campaign title, update date, targeted industries, and associated malware. Users can follow specific campaigns for ongoing updates.

This feature is designed to enhance situational awareness and improve threat mitigation strategies for security professionals. For more information, visit the [Google Threat Intelligence Campaigns documentation](https://gtidocs.virustotal.com/docs/campaigns).

# Campaigns Security teams may struggle with understanding their threat landscape and determining what activity to focus on, especially when faced with a myriad of emerging threats. To better assist these teams, the Threat Campaign feature in Google Threat Intelligence gives security professionals visibility into active campaigns affecting their industries, regions, and peers. This feature provides a campaign-centric view and lets you easily pivot across all accompanying intelligence. Campaigns help you better prioritize mitigation and response actions in preparation for the next attack with a high degree of confidence. Data is updated daily as analysts get new campaign details, so you get access to Google Threat Intelligence’s knowledge in near real-time. The Explore Campaigns dashboard. 
## Campaign Creation
 Google TI analyzes emerging threat data directly from client engagements and from open source or other collections methods when assessing a campaign's viability for a Threat Campaign profile. Considerations for campaign creation ... * Who is the threat actor, what are their motivations, and how impactful are their operations on targeted organizations? For example, an espionage actor targeting Ministries of Foreign Affairs or a fast-paced extortion operator does not equate to automated drive-by adware or infostealer campaigns. ... we have a sufficient amount of shareable, actionable data that we can disseminate to our clients? ... available to share? We take sensitivities of this data very seriously and make every effort to restrict the unintended release of sensitive, client-attributable data into the Threat Campaign profile. ## Campaign List
 All active campaigns appear in full in the Campaign List. You can search or filter on the results and open a specific campaign to access its more granular details. Campaigns are automatically updated as analysts tag events. You can use various filters to pare down the list:
* Creation Date
* Last Modification Date
* Origin
* Source Region * Targeted Region
* Targeted Industry
* Associated Malware
* Associated Tools## Campaign card
 Each campaign is shown as a card in the list with relevant information. A modal displays a sample Campaign Summary. 
1. Campaign title 2. Update date: Date when the campaign card was last updated with any new associated report, malware family, targeted ... 3. Owner: Creator of the campaign card.
4. Campaign ID ... 6. Description: Short summary of the Campaign
7. Targeted Industries
8. Targeted Regions
9. Associated Actors ... #### Follow Campaigns
 In the**Campaigns**tab, click**Follow**for any Campaign to monitor changes to the selected Campaigns over time, such as ... Follow campaign buttons To add a Campaign to a Threat Profile, open the card details and use the Follow button inside, it will give you the option to choose which profile to add it to. Malware Follow and Add to Threat profile 
#### Run TTP analysis
 You are redirected to TTP Analysis tab where a MITRE ATT&ACK matrix is filled with the TTPs of the ... * Clicking on the name of a Technique or Tactic will redirect you to a Intelligene search filtering for that specific Technique (example: attack\_technique:T1129 ) ... Campaign's IOCs that match this Technique## Campaign details 
Click on the name of any Campaign card for a view its details, navigate its tabs for specific data: * **Summary**: This tab displays the same comprehensive summary of the Campaign as seen in the quick view. It also includes an Overview block with information such as Targeted Regions and Industries and dates on campaigns's activity. results.title: Scan History - Google Threat Intelligence
results.summary:
The webpage on "Scan History" from Google Threat Intelligence provides a feature that allows users to visualize changes to an entity over time. This tool is useful for identifying inactive issues and tracking changes in technologies. Users can access the Scan History by selecting an entity and navigating to the corresponding tab, where they can view up to the last 25 scans. Changes are highlighted in green, while unchanged scans are grayed out. The default view shows the most recent scan with changes, and users can compare it with previous scans by selecting specific date tiles or using navigation arrows. 

For information specifically related to the Gemini platform, please clarify your query, as this page does not directly address Gemini.

# Scan History
This feature lets you visualize changes to an Entity over time. Some use cases include:
* Identifying Issues that have gone inactive.
* Identifying Technologies that have changed. To access Scan History information, select an Entity and navigate to the**Scan History**tab. Scan History comparison of differences in an Entity on two different dates. Scans where the Entity was seen are listed as tiles. Up to the last 25 scans are provided. Scans where changes to the Entity were observed are represented in green. Grayed scans indicate that the Entity was unchanged for the given date. The**Previous Scans**section defaults to the most recent scan where a change to the Entity was observed, provided that date differs from the**Current Scan**. You can select any previous scan to compare differences to the**Current Scan**. Select a specific date tile, or use the arrow buttons to toggle through the scans. **Updated3 days ago

results.title: Agentic User Guide - Google Threat Intelligence - VirusTotal
results.summary: The "Agentic User Guide" on VirusTotal provides detailed instructions on using the Agentic Platform, which leverages Google Threat Intelligence and Large Language Models to enhance cybersecurity investigations. Users can interact with AI agents to analyze threats, generate reports, and retrieve information on Indicators of Compromise (IoCs). Key features include the ability to ask specific cybersecurity questions, generate executive summaries, and perform in-depth malware analysis. The platform also allows users to save prompts for recurring tasks and share sessions with colleagues for collaborative analysis. For more information, visit the guide [here](https://gtidocs.virustotal.com/docs/agentic-user-g).

results.title: September 26th, 2024 - Gemini AI and Dark Web Monitoring improvements
results.summary:
The webpage discusses updates to Gemini AI and Dark Web Monitoring as of September 26, 2024. Key improvements include:

1. **Artificial Intelligence Enhancements**:
   - **Code Insight Expansion**: Gemini AI now supports additional file formats (Batch, Shell, VBScript, Office documents) for malware analysis, enabling quicker and more comprehensive insights.
   - **Natural Language Search**: Users can now ask natural language questions to retrieve information about Indicators of Compromise (IoCs) and threats, enhancing the search experience.
   - **Threat Article Summarization**: Gemini AI automates the ingestion and summarization of online threat articles, extracting key entities and contextualizing IoCs for better threat intelligence.

2. **Dark Web Monitoring Improvements**:
   - **Data Leak Monitoring**: The Digital Threat Monitoring (DTM) module has been enhanced to detect leaks of sensitive information, helping organizations monitor for threats like brand impersonation and compromised credentials.

These updates aim to improve threat detection and analysis capabilities for users, making it easier to navigate and respond to emerging cyber threats.
highlights: Code Insight] is a cutting-edge feature powered by Gemini AI that leverages artificial intelligence for code analysis. It is a malware analyst/reverse engineer assistant that produces natural language summaries of file capabilities and intent. We’ve extended it to support more file formats such as Batch, Shell, VBScript, Office documents and more. We are also experimenting with Windows Executables, example - Code Insight was able to reverse engineer and analyze the decompiled code of the WannaCry malware in a single pass — and identify the killswitch — in only 34 seconds. ... codeinsight:keylogger] . Gemini AI Search - Google Threat Intelligence’s search capabilities allow users to look for any particular IoC (file hash, domain, URL or IP address), IoCs matching certain static/dynamic/reputational/code criteria and high order threats (actors, malware, campaigns, vulnerabilities, etc.). We’ve extended search so that users can ask natural language questions to get a generative AI-powered overview of a topic based on our Google Threat Intelligence curated knowledge. You can even interact with Gemini and ask follow up questions on any given subject. ... Online threat articles summarization and entity extraction. Google Threat Intelligence is all about providing the deepest and broadest knowledge on threats. Our Mandiant experts produce curated finished intelligence based on differentiated frontline visibility into breaches, at the same time, we ingest 3rd-party articles and any kind of online references through direct connection to the Google crawler. We are now leveraging Gemini AI to automatically ingest, label and summarize OSINT articles to reduce time to investigate and create actionable threat intelligence research. As we identify and ingest articles, we automatically extract and index notions such as: related actors, source regions, targeted regions, targeted industries, motivations, etc. This information enters our knowledge base and becomes searchable, and, at the same time, it automatically contextualizes any IoCs that ... ## Dark web monitoring Dark web data leak expansion. Digital Threat Monitoring (DTM) is a dark web monitoring Google Threat Intelligence module to help customers identify emerging threats in hard to reach (typically inaccessible) places on the Internet. DTM allows you to define and monitor certain threat scenarios such as impersonation of your brand, compromised credentials, supply chain compromise, etc . We’ve extended DTM with a data leaks monitor allowing you to detect exposure of your sensitive information such as financial data, trade secrets, or customer information. This monitor also acts on the daily 2M+ VirusTotal file submissions , allowing you to identify exposures ... monitors for leaked usernames and passwords across the deep, dark, web. Enhancements to credential monitoring matching logic. In the aforementioned DTM module , we have made some critical enhancements to our credential monitoring logic.
